---
title: "Language, Semantics, and Formal Shape"
---

# Language, Semantics, and Formal Shape

Range: `0113-0249`.

## Completion

| Status      | Count |
| ----------- | ----- |
| authored    | 20    |
| stub        | 114   |
| needs_sense | 3     |

Use the summary table for scanning. Each row links to the detailed anchored entry below.

| Sigil | Term                                          | Completion  | Summary                                                                             |
| ----- | --------------------------------------------- | ----------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 0113  | `STUB` [abstract class](#rune-0113)           | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0114  | `STUB` [alias](#rune-0114)                    | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0115  | `STUB` [annotation](#rune-0115)               | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0116  | `STUB` [argument](#rune-0116)                 | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0117  | `STUB` [arity](#rune-0117)                    | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0118  | `STUB` [assembly](#rune-0118)                 | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0119  | `STUB` [assertion {Lang}](#rune-0119)         | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0120  | [AST](#rune-0120)                             | authored    | The tree form of parsed program structure.                                          |
| 0121  | `STUB` [attribute](#rune-0121)                | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0122  | `STUB` [binding](#rune-0122)                  | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0123  | `STUB` [borrow {Lang}](#rune-0123)            | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0124  | [borrow checker](#rune-0124)                  | authored    | A checker that enforces safe borrowing and lifetime law before runtime.             |
| 0125  | `STUB` [bytecode](#rune-0125)                 | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0126  | `STUB` [capture](#rune-0126)                  | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0127  | `STUB` [cast](#rune-0127)                     | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0128  | [class](#rune-0128)                           | needs_sense | A shape word for bundled state and behavior.                                        |
| 0129  | [closure](#rune-0129)                         | needs_sense | A capture word; it carries surrounding bindings forward with executable intent.     |
| 0130  | `STUB` [coercion](#rune-0130)                 | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0131  | `STUB` [comment](#rune-0131)                  | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0132  | [compile-time](#rune-0132)                    | authored    | Known and fixed before execution begins.                                            |
| 0133  | [compiler](#rune-0133)                        | authored    | The translator from source language to lower-level executable form.                 |
| 0134  | `STUB` [constant](#rune-0134)                 | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0135  | `STUB` [constraint {Lang}](#rune-0135)        | stub        | A bounding word; it removes degrees of freedom in order to improve correctness.     |
| 0136  | `STUB` [constructor](#rune-0136)              | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0137  | `STUB` [contravariance](#rune-0137)           | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0138  | `STUB` [covariance](#rune-0138)               | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0139  | `STUB` [currying](#rune-0139)                 | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0140  | `STUB` [declaration](#rune-0140)              | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0141  | `STUB` [decorator {Lang}](#rune-0141)         | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0142  | `STUB` [default](#rune-0142)                  | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0143  | `STUB` [definition](#rune-0143)               | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0144  | `STUB` [denotation](#rune-0144)               | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0145  | `STUB` [destructor {Lang}](#rune-0145)        | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0146  | `STUB` [dispatch {Lang}](#rune-0146)          | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0147  | `STUB` [domain-specific language](#rune-0147) | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0148  | `STUB` [dynamic scope](#rune-0148)            | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0149  | `STUB` [effect](#rune-0149)                   | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0150  | `STUB` [enum](#rune-0150)                     | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0151  | `STUB` [evaluation {Lang}](#rune-0151)        | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0152  | `STUB` [export {Lang}](#rune-0152)            | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0153  | `STUB` [expression](#rune-0153)               | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0154  | `STUB` [extern](#rune-0154)                   | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0155  | `STUB` [field {Lang}](#rune-0155)             | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0156  | `STUB` [final](#rune-0156)                    | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0157  | [function](#rune-0157)                        | needs_sense | A callable word; given inputs, it yields outputs by a named rule.                   |
| 0158  | [generic {Lang}](#rune-0158)                  | authored    | One definition parameterized over many concrete types or cases.                     |
| 0159  | [grammar](#rune-0159)                         | authored    | The rules that say what strings count as well-formed structure.                     |
| 0160  | `STUB` [higher-order function](#rune-0160)    | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0161  | `STUB` [identifier](#rune-0161)               | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0162  | `STUB` [impl](#rune-0162)                     | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0163  | `STUB` [implementation {Lang}](#rune-0163)    | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0164  | `STUB` [import](#rune-0164)                   | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0165  | `STUB` [include](#rune-0165)                  | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0166  | [inference](#rune-0166)                       | authored    | Recovering missing type or shape information from surrounding evidence.             |
| 0167  | `STUB` [inheritance](#rune-0167)              | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0168  | `STUB` [inline {Lang}](#rune-0168)            | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0169  | `STUB` [instruction {Lang}](#rune-0169)       | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0170  | [interface {Lang}](#rune-0170)                | authored    | A boundary word; it lets different bodies touch through a disciplined seam.         |
| 0171  | [interpreter {Lang}](#rune-0171)              | authored    | A runtime that executes a representation rather than precompiling it away.          |
| 0172  | [IR](#rune-0172)                              | authored    | An internal representation built for analysis and transformation.                   |
| 0173  | `STUB` [keyword](#rune-0173)                  | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0174  | `STUB` [lambda](#rune-0174)                   | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0175  | `STUB` [lexical scope](#rune-0175)            | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0176  | `STUB` [lifetime {Lang}](#rune-0176)          | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0177  | [linker](#rune-0177)                          | authored    | The binder that turns compiled pieces into one loadable whole.                      |
| 0178  | `STUB` [literal](#rune-0178)                  | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0179  | `STUB` [loader](#rune-0179)                   | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0180  | [macro](#rune-0180)                           | authored    | Code that writes or reshapes code.                                                  |
| 0181  | `STUB` [metaprogramming](#rune-0181)          | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0182  | `STUB` [method](#rune-0182)                   | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0183  | `STUB` [mixin](#rune-0183)                    | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0184  | `STUB` [module {Lang}](#rune-0184)            | stub        | A containment word; related definitions are packaged as one coherent unit.          |
| 0185  | `STUB` [monomorphization](#rune-0185)         | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0186  | `STUB` [mutability](#rune-0186)               | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0187  | `STUB` [name resolution](#rune-0187)          | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0188  | [namespace {Lang}](#rune-0188)                | authored    | A naming region that prevents collisions and clarifies ownership.                   |
| 0189  | `STUB` [object](#rune-0189)                   | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0190  | `STUB` [opcode {Lang}](#rune-0190)            | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0191  | `STUB` [overload](#rune-0191)                 | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0192  | `STUB` [override](#rune-0192)                 | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0193  | `STUB` [ownership](#rune-0193)                | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0194  | `STUB` [package {Lang}](#rune-0194)           | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0195  | `STUB` [parameter](#rune-0195)                | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0196  | `STUB` [parametric polymorphism](#rune-0196)  | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0197  | [parser](#rune-0197)                          | authored    | The mechanism that turns surface text into structured meaning.                      |
| 0198  | `STUB` [pattern](#rune-0198)                  | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0199  | `STUB` [pattern matching](#rune-0199)         | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0200  | [polymorphism](#rune-0200)                    | authored    | One interface, many concrete behaviors.                                             |
| 0201  | `STUB` [procedure {Lang}](#rune-0201)         | stub        | A method word; not just what to do, but in what order to do it.                     |
| 0202  | `STUB` [program](#rune-0202)                  | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0203  | `STUB` [property {Lang}](#rune-0203)          | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0204  | `STUB` [protected](#rune-0204)                | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0205  | `STUB` [public](#rune-0205)                   | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0206  | `STUB` [purity](#rune-0206)                   | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0207  | `STUB` [qualified name](#rune-0207)           | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0208  | `STUB` [readonly](#rune-0208)                 | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0209  | `STUB` [record {Lang}](#rune-0209)            | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0210  | `STUB` [reference {Lang}](#rune-0210)         | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0211  | `STUB` [referential transparency](#rune-0211) | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0212  | `STUB` [reflection](#rune-0212)               | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0213  | `STUB` [resolution](#rune-0213)               | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0214  | `STUB` [return type](#rune-0214)              | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0215  | `STUB` [runtime type](#rune-0215)             | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0216  | `STUB` [scope {Lang}](#rune-0216)             | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0217  | `STUB` [sealed](#rune-0217)                   | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0218  | [semantics](#rune-0218)                       | authored    | What a program means, not just how it is spelled.                                   |
| 0219  | [signature {Lang}](#rune-0219)                | authored    | A proving word; identity or contract is made checkable.                             |
| 0220  | `STUB` [source code](#rune-0220)              | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0221  | `STUB` [specialization](#rune-0221)           | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0222  | `STUB` [statement](#rune-0222)                | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0223  | `STUB` [static dispatch](#rune-0223)          | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0224  | `STUB` [strictness](#rune-0224)               | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0225  | `STUB` [struct {Lang}](#rune-0225)            | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0226  | `STUB` [subtype](#rune-0226)                  | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0227  | `STUB` [supertype](#rune-0227)                | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0228  | `STUB` [symbol](#rune-0228)                   | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0229  | [syntax](#rune-0229)                          | authored    | The legal surface form of the language.                                             |
| 0230  | `STUB` [template {Lang}](#rune-0230)          | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0231  | `STUB` [theorem {Lang}](#rune-0231)           | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0232  | `STUB` [token {Lang}](#rune-0232)             | stub        | A countable unit of text or symbol used for parsing, modeling, or encoding.         |
| 0233  | `STUB` [trait](#rune-0233)                    | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0234  | `STUB` [transpiler](#rune-0234)               | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0235  | `STUB` [tuple {Lang}](#rune-0235)             | stub        | A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.           |
| 0236  | [type](#rune-0236)                            | authored    | A declared or inferred class of values and allowed operations.                      |
| 0237  | `STUB` [type alias](#rune-0237)               | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0238  | `STUB` [type inference](#rune-0238)           | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0239  | `STUB` [type system](#rune-0239)              | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0240  | [typechecker](#rune-0240)                     | authored    | The checker that enforces type law before execution.                                |
| 0241  | `STUB` [typeclass](#rune-0241)                | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0242  | `STUB` [unification](#rune-0242)              | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0243  | `STUB` [union](#rune-0243)                    | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0244  | `STUB` [value](#rune-0244)                    | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0245  | `STUB` [variance](#rune-0245)                 | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |
| 0246  | `STUB` [virtual](#rune-0246)                  | stub        | A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about. |
| 0247  | `STUB` [visibility](#rune-0247)               | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0248  | `STUB` [visitor](#rune-0248)                  | stub        | A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.          |
| 0249  | `STUB` [where-clause](#rune-0249)             | stub        | A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.                     |

## Entries

### [0113] abstract class {#rune-0113}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0114] alias {#rune-0114}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0115] annotation {#rune-0115}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0116] argument {#rune-0116}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0117] arity {#rune-0117}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0118] assembly {#rune-0118}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0119] assertion {Lang} {#rune-0119}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0120] AST {#rune-0120}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The tree form of parsed program structure.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

**Pocket gloss:** The tree form of parsed program structure.

### [0121] attribute {#rune-0121}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0122] binding {#rune-0122}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0123] borrow {Lang} {#rune-0123}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0124] borrow checker {#rune-0124}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A checker that enforces safe borrowing and lifetime law before runtime.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

**Pocket gloss:** A checker that enforces safe borrowing and lifetime law before runtime.

### [0125] bytecode {#rune-0125}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0126] capture {#rune-0126}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0127] cast {#rune-0127}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0128] class {#rune-0128}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** needs_sense

**Force:** A shape word for bundled state and behavior.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0129] closure {#rune-0129}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** needs_sense

**Force:** A capture word; it carries surrounding bindings forward with executable intent.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0130] coercion {#rune-0130}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0131] comment {#rune-0131}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0132] compile-time {#rune-0132}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Known and fixed before execution begins.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

**Pocket gloss:** Known and fixed before execution begins.

### [0133] compiler {#rune-0133}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The translator from source language to lower-level executable form.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

**Pocket gloss:** The translator from source language to lower-level executable form.

### [0134] constant {#rune-0134}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0135] constraint {Lang} {#rune-0135}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A bounding word; it removes degrees of freedom in order to improve correctness.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0136] constructor {#rune-0136}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0137] contravariance {#rune-0137}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0138] covariance {#rune-0138}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0139] currying {#rune-0139}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0140] declaration {#rune-0140}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0141] decorator {Lang} {#rune-0141}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0142] default {#rune-0142}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0143] definition {#rune-0143}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0144] denotation {#rune-0144}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0145] destructor {Lang} {#rune-0145}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0146] dispatch {Lang} {#rune-0146}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0147] domain-specific language {#rune-0147}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0148] dynamic scope {#rune-0148}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0149] effect {#rune-0149}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0150] enum {#rune-0150}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0151] evaluation {Lang} {#rune-0151}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0152] export {Lang} {#rune-0152}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0153] expression {#rune-0153}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0154] extern {#rune-0154}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0155] field {Lang} {#rune-0155}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0156] final {#rune-0156}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0157] function {#rune-0157}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** needs_sense

**Force:** A callable word; given inputs, it yields outputs by a named rule.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0158] generic {Lang} {#rune-0158}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** One definition parameterized over many concrete types or cases.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

**Pocket gloss:** One definition parameterized over many concrete types or cases.

### [0159] grammar {#rune-0159}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The rules that say what strings count as well-formed structure.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

**Pocket gloss:** The rules that say what strings count as well-formed structure.

### [0160] higher-order function {#rune-0160}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0161] identifier {#rune-0161}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0162] impl {#rune-0162}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0163] implementation {Lang} {#rune-0163}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0164] import {#rune-0164}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0165] include {#rune-0165}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0166] inference {#rune-0166}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Recovering missing type or shape information from surrounding evidence.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

**Pocket gloss:** Recovering missing type or shape information from surrounding evidence.

### [0167] inheritance {#rune-0167}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0168] inline {Lang} {#rune-0168}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0169] instruction {Lang} {#rune-0169}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0170] interface {Lang} {#rune-0170}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A boundary word; it lets different bodies touch through a disciplined seam.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0171] interpreter {Lang} {#rune-0171}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A runtime that executes a representation rather than precompiling it away.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

**Pocket gloss:** A runtime that executes a representation rather than precompiling it away.

### [0172] IR {#rune-0172}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** An internal representation built for analysis and transformation.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

**Pocket gloss:** An internal representation built for analysis and transformation.

### [0173] keyword {#rune-0173}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0174] lambda {#rune-0174}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0175] lexical scope {#rune-0175}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0176] lifetime {Lang} {#rune-0176}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0177] linker {#rune-0177}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The binder that turns compiled pieces into one loadable whole.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

**Pocket gloss:** The binder that turns compiled pieces into one loadable whole.

### [0178] literal {#rune-0178}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0179] loader {#rune-0179}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0180] macro {#rune-0180}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Code that writes or reshapes code.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

**Pocket gloss:** Code that writes or reshapes code.

### [0181] metaprogramming {#rune-0181}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0182] method {#rune-0182}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0183] mixin {#rune-0183}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0184] module {Lang} {#rune-0184}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A containment word; related definitions are packaged as one coherent unit.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0185] monomorphization {#rune-0185}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0186] mutability {#rune-0186}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0187] name resolution {#rune-0187}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0188] namespace {Lang} {#rune-0188}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A naming region that prevents collisions and clarifies ownership.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

**Pocket gloss:** A naming region that prevents collisions and clarifies ownership.

### [0189] object {#rune-0189}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0190] opcode {Lang} {#rune-0190}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0191] overload {#rune-0191}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0192] override {#rune-0192}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0193] ownership {#rune-0193}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0194] package {Lang} {#rune-0194}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0195] parameter {#rune-0195}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0196] parametric polymorphism {#rune-0196}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0197] parser {#rune-0197}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The mechanism that turns surface text into structured meaning.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

**Pocket gloss:** The mechanism that turns surface text into structured meaning.

### [0198] pattern {#rune-0198}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0199] pattern matching {#rune-0199}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0200] polymorphism {#rune-0200}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** One interface, many concrete behaviors.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

**Pocket gloss:** One interface, many concrete behaviors.

### [0201] procedure {Lang} {#rune-0201}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A method word; not just what to do, but in what order to do it.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0202] program {#rune-0202}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0203] property {Lang} {#rune-0203}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0204] protected {#rune-0204}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0205] public {#rune-0205}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0206] purity {#rune-0206}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0207] qualified name {#rune-0207}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0208] readonly {#rune-0208}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0209] record {Lang} {#rune-0209}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0210] reference {Lang} {#rune-0210}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0211] referential transparency {#rune-0211}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0212] reflection {#rune-0212}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0213] resolution {#rune-0213}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0214] return type {#rune-0214}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0215] runtime type {#rune-0215}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0216] scope {Lang} {#rune-0216}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0217] sealed {#rune-0217}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0218] semantics {#rune-0218}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** What a program means, not just how it is spelled.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

**Pocket gloss:** What a program means, not just how it is spelled.

### [0219] signature {Lang} {#rune-0219}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A proving word; identity or contract is made checkable.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0220] source code {#rune-0220}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0221] specialization {#rune-0221}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0222] statement {#rune-0222}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0223] static dispatch {#rune-0223}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0224] strictness {#rune-0224}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0225] struct {Lang} {#rune-0225}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0226] subtype {#rune-0226}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0227] supertype {#rune-0227}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0228] symbol {#rune-0228}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0229] syntax {#rune-0229}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The legal surface form of the language.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

**Pocket gloss:** The legal surface form of the language.

### [0230] template {Lang} {#rune-0230}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0231] theorem {Lang} {#rune-0231}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0232] token {Lang} {#rune-0232}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A countable unit of text or symbol used for parsing, modeling, or encoding.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0233] trait {#rune-0233}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0234] transpiler {#rune-0234}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0235] tuple {Lang} {#rune-0235}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A semantics word; it alters what symbols are allowed to mean in practice.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0236] type {#rune-0236}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A declared or inferred class of values and allowed operations.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

**Pocket gloss:** A declared or inferred class of values and allowed operations.

### [0237] type alias {#rune-0237}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0238] type inference {#rune-0238}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0239] type system {#rune-0239}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0240] typechecker {#rune-0240}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The checker that enforces type law before execution.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

**Pocket gloss:** The checker that enforces type law before execution.

### [0241] typeclass {#rune-0241}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0242] unification {#rune-0242}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0243] union {#rune-0243}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0244] value {#rune-0244}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0245] variance {#rune-0245}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0246] virtual {#rune-0246}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A shape-of-thought word; it influences how programs are written and reasoned about.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0247] visibility {#rune-0247}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0248] visitor {#rune-0248}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A grammar-word; it shapes how meaning is expressed to humans and machines.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.

### [0249] where-clause {#rune-0249}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A law-of-form word; it changes what the language can guarantee.

**Shadow:** the form can look precise while still carrying ambiguous or invalid meaning.
